ingredients.name: Coffee OB
ingredients.localName: カフェOB 越谷駅前店
ingredients.address: 第３JMビル １F, ３-1 Akayamahoncho, Koshigaya, Saitama 343-0808, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Coffee OB near Koshigaya Station's west exit is documented in pilgrimage guides as a café whose storefront appears in street-level background shots of the characters' Oborozuka neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Art Object of a Cloud: Light
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒135-0063 Tokyo, Koto City, Ariake, 3 Chome−11−1, 東京ビッグサイト屋上展示場
ingredients.entityContext: This sculpture on the Tokyo Big Sight grounds is visible in the approach and exterior shots of the Comiket episode (episode 7), part of the faithful rendering of the real convention venue that KyoAni used as the basis for the event.

ingredients.name: Kasama Station
ingredients.entityContext: Kasama Station is confirmed as the model for the rural station near Kobayashi's family home, which Kobayashi, Tohru, and Kanna visit in the emotional season 1 finale. It's the arrival point fans head to when retracing that family-reunion journey.

ingredients.name: Ningyocho Station
ingredients.entityContext: The Ningyocho area is the stage for Ilulu's dramatic entry in Dragon Maid S episode 1, where the Chaos-Faction dragon confronts Kobayashi in the streets near this subway station. The Nihonbashi streetscape visible behind Ilulu's transformation matches the real exit.

ingredients.name: Mr. Donut Ekia Koshigaya Shop
ingredients.localName: ミスタードーナツ エキア越谷ショップ
ingredients.entityContext: This Mister Donut inside the EQUiA mall connected to Koshigaya Station appears in background shots of the station area and is a stop on the fan pilgrimage route around Koshigaya's Oborozuka Station surroundings.

ingredients.name: McDonald's EQUiA Koshigaya Shop
ingredients.localName: マクドナルド エキア越谷店
ingredients.type: ファースト フード店
categories: ファースト フード店

ingredients.name: French Cuisine Pomme de Pain
ingredients.localName: フランス料理ポーム・ド・パン

ingredients.name: Higashikoshigaya Hatchome Keyaki Park
ingredients.localName: 東越谷八丁目けやき公園
ingredients.entityContext: This keyaki (zelkova) tree park in Higashi-Koshigaya is one of the neighborhood parks where Kanna plays outdoors, its tree-lined paths closely matching the anime's scenes of the characters spending time in the local green spaces.

ingredients.name: Higashikoshigaya Button Park
ingredients.entityContext: Higashikoshigaya Button Park is among the Higashi-Koshigaya neighborhood parks documented in Dragon Maid pilgrimage guides, where the residential streets and open playground areas appear as background in the characters' daily life scenes.

ingredients.name: Yanagiyu Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: Yanagiyu Bridge is one of the charming stone bridges crossing Kinosaki Onsen's willow-lined canal, appearing in the hot-spring trip episode alongside the ryokan inns and street scenes that establish the town's atmosphere.

ingredients.name: Tokyo Big Sight
ingredients.type: コンベンション センター
categories: コンベンション センター
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-11-1 Ariake, Koto City, Tokyo 135-0063, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tokyo Big Sight hosts the Comiket convention prominently depicted in episode 7, where Kobayashi helps Makoto sell doujinshi and Fafnir sets up his own booth — KyoAni faithfully recreated the hall interiors and the building's iconic inverted-pyramid exterior.

ingredients.name: Higashikoshigaya Nanachome Shiinoki Park
ingredients.localName: 東越谷七丁目しいの木公園
ingredients.entityContext: Shiinoki Park in Higashi-Koshigaya 7-chome is another of the quiet residential parks that form the backdrop of Kanna and her friends' after-school wanderings, part of the walkable pilgrimage cluster in the Higashi-Koshigaya neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Higashi-Koshigaya 2-chome monthly pay parking lot
ingredients.localName: 東越谷二丁目月極有料駐車場

ingredients.name: Rotisseries
ingredients.localName: チキンプレイスロティサリｰズ 東日本橋店

ingredients.name: Benkei from “Kanjincho”
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-36-13 Nihonbashiningyocho, Chuo City, Tokyo 103-0013, Japan

ingredients.name: Sukeroku
ingredients.localName: かにちらし寿司 すけ六
ingredients.entityContext: This sushi restaurant on Kinosaki Onsen's main street is part of the pilgrimage cluster from the hot-spring trip episode, situated along the willow-lined canal that the characters walk in the episode's town-exploration scenes.

ingredients.name: Sasaki Liquor Store
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒103-0013 Tokyo, Chuo City, Nihonbashiningyocho, 2 Chome−20−3
ingredients.entityContext: This old-fashioned sake shop in Nihonbashi Ningyocho appears in the background streetscape of Dragon Maid S, part of the Ningyocho district backgrounds that KyoAni depicted during Ilulu's introduction arc.

ingredients.name: Heiwa Bridge
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-3-16 Higashitateishi, Katsushika City, Tokyo 124-0013, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Heiwa Bridge over the Motoarakawa river appears as a recurring background crossing in Kobayashi and Tohru's neighborhood strolls, and is one of the bridges documented in fan pilgrimage guides to the Higashi-Koshigaya area.

ingredients.name: Kasumi
ingredients.localName: カスミ フードスクエア 越谷ツインシティ店

ingredients.name: Chikamoriinari Shrine
ingredients.entityContext: Chikamoriinari Shrine in Kasama is located near the model for Kobayashi's parents' neighborhood, and appears in pilgrimage guides for the Kasama area visited in the season 1 finale.

ingredients.name: Genshoin
ingredients.entityContext: Genshoin temple is in the Kasama area that models Kobayashi's family hometown, the rural setting of the moving finale episode where Kobayashi brings Tohru and Kanna home to meet her parents.

ingredients.name: Hatsukoshigayaekihigashiguchiten Dry Cleaners
ingredients.localName: クリーニングハーツ越谷駅東口店

ingredients.name: Hankoyasan 21 Koshigaya Shiyakusho Maeten
ingredients.localName: はんこ屋さん21 越谷市役所前店

ingredients.name: Imamura
ingredients.address: 1-13 Oshimakamicho, Kawasaki Ward, Kawasaki, Kanagawa 210-0836, Japan

ingredients.name: Chaya
ingredients.localName: 城崎ジェラートカフェChaya
ingredients.entityContext: The gelato café Chaya in Kinosaki Onsen is where Elma eats the famous hot-spring buns (onsen manju) during the episode 14 / OVA hot-spring trip. The café's outdoor foot bath, where visitors can soak while eating gelato, is the scene fans re-enact on their visit.

ingredients.name: KOSHIGAYA STATION
ingredients.entityContext: Koshigaya Station is the real-world model for 'Oborozuka Station,' the fictional hub of Kobayashi and Tohru's neighborhood. Fans walk out of this exact station exit into the streets that inspired the anime's everyday commuter scenes.

ingredients.name: Chinese Cuisine Taoyuan
ingredients.address: 中華料理 桃園 4-1 Akayamahoncho, Koshigaya, Saitama 343-0808
ingredients.entityContext: This Chinese restaurant near Koshigaya Station's west exit is documented in pilgrimage guides as part of the Oborozuka station-front streetscape depicted in the anime's everyday slice-of-life backgrounds.

ingredients.name: Hair color specialty store STORY Koshigaya
ingredients.localName: ヘアカラー専門店 STORY 越谷店

ingredients.name: QUEEN BURGER
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒343-0808 Saitama, Koshigaya, Akayamahoncho, 17−29 野中PLAZA

ingredients.name: Saitama Resona Bank Koshigaya
ingredients.localName: 埼玉りそな銀行 越谷支店

ingredients.name: Kinosaki Yamamotoya
ingredients.address: 名古屋 うまいもん通り 広小路口, 1 Chome-1-4 Meieki, Nakamura Ward, Nagoya, Aichi 450-0002, Japan

ingredients.name: Public onsen fountain
ingredients.entityContext: This public drinking-water spring (飲泉場) in Kinosaki Onsen appears in the hot-spring episode as part of the town's streetscape, where characters stroll the canal-side path between the seven bathhouses.

ingredients.name: Casa Cefiro
ingredients.localName: カーサ・ セフィーロ
